identity.canonical_title: Q-DEFORMED SYMMETRY GROUP Canonical Statutory Formula

theory.canonical_statement: [ID 988] q-DEFORMED SYMMETRY GROUP Canonical Statutory Formula: $SU_q(2)_{TZP} \subseteq \text{Aut}(TZP)$ Formal Technical Dissertation: The symmetry group governing the bifurcation point. It is a quantum deformation ($q$-deformed) of the special unitary group $SU(2)$, describing the non-commutative geometry at the singularity. Source: [7]
theory.technical_interpretation: Q-DEFORMED SYMMETRY GROUP Canonical Statutory Formula is a symbolic expression, written SU_q(2)_TZP subseteq Aut(TZP). It introduces a symbol or relation used elsewhere in the framework. It is built from S, U_q, T, Z, P. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.
